name: Steamed Fish with Fish Gravy
recipeIngredient: Whole red snapper (or similar fish, 2 to 4 pounds, gutted and split with head on)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the fish with lime juice and rinse, drain, and pat dry.
2. Make diagonal gashes on each side of the fish toward the head.
3. Mix salt, pepper, garlic (2 cloves), vinegar, and hot pepper to form a paste.
4. Rub the paste onto the gashes and inside surfaces of the fish.
5. Place the fish in a flat glass baking dish with onions (reserve some), green pepper, remaining garlic, and hot pepper. Marinate for one hour.
6. Shell shrimp, reserving shells; chop shrimp and set aside.
7. In a large saucepan, simmer water, reserved onion, celery, and fish head with shell. Cook 15 minutes.
8. Heat oil in a large cast-iron skillet; remove marinade from fish and dredge in flour.
9. Fry quickly for 3 to 4 minutes per side until golden brown but not cooked through.
10. Lower heat and add remaining marinade vegetables, simmering gently for 15 to 20 minutes.
11. Remove fish and vegetables with a slotted spoon to serving dish; keep warm.
12. In skillet, add 2 tablespoons flour and cook slowly, scraping bottom, until gravy thickens.
13. Strain gravy and return to clean skillet; add shrimp and cook until pink and gravy is thick.
14. Serve shrimp gravy over fish.
Serves 4 to 6.
From the "Family of the Spirit Cookbook: Recipes and Remembrances from African-American Kitchens," by John Pinderhughes (Simon and Schuster, 1990). Snapper can be substituted with Hawaiian varieties like onaga, opakapaka, or ta'ape, or Pacific rockfish.
